mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single month, it signals a rapid labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real personal consumption expenditures indicates a synchronized demand-supply mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical co-occurrence events, or cross-sectional momentum dominates the macro signal.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing-led expansion phase, sustained growth in housing starts reinforces consumer spending momentum, driving positive equity returns over the next month as construction and related sectors outperform.
failure_condition: Forward returns in housing-led expansions do not significantly outperform baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal flips direction when housing starts decline.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent while real consumer spending simultaneously falls, the combination of restrictive policy and weakening demand forces a sharp repricing of equity risk premiums, producing negative forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with demand weakness
failure_condition: The joint threshold signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical co-occurrence events.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and operational efficiency, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in rising INDPRO periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with declining real consumer spending indicates a broad-based economic slowdown that compresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of historical co-occurrence events, or cross-sectional value factors dominate the macro signal.

mechanism_narrative: When real consumer spending falls below its 3-month moving average while housing starts decline simultaneously, it signals a sharp pullback in aggregate demand that triggers negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS < 3mo MA and HOUST declines month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint threshold signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical co-occurrence events.
